summary: Score up to 4.0/5 — first time hitting this milestone. All 3 high-priority accessibility issues from #18 are FIXED (confetti reduced-motion, keyboard speech bubbles, keyboard tooltips). All characters now have tabIndex={0} + focus/blur handlers. Dead CSS purge appears done. The codebase is genuinely solid now. Remaining issues are mostly about polish — timestamps not ticking, some code dedup, and mobile responsiveness gaps. The fun factor remains excellent.

suggestions.title: Activity log relative timestamps never update
suggestions.description: App.jsx getRelativeTime() is called only at render. If the dashboard stays open, '2m ago' stays frozen forever. This has been flagged since review #18 as medium but promoting to high — it's a visible user-facing bug. Fix: add a 60s interval that bumps a tick state to force re-renders of timestamp components.

suggestions.title: Office idle duration timestamps also never update
suggestions.description: Office.jsx formatIdleDuration() is pure — called once at render. Same frozen-timestamp bug as the activity log. An idle worker showing '5m' will show '5m' forever. Fix: share a global tick context or add a local interval in the Character component.

suggestions.title: Office view not usable on small screens
suggestions.description: The 2D office layout uses absolute positioning with percentage-based left/top values. On screens under ~600px wide, characters overlap and nameplates become unreadable. No media query adjusts the office floor layout for mobile. Consider: stack characters vertically on mobile, or show a simplified list view.

suggestions.title: MANAGER_QUOTES array is redundant with VIBE_QUOTES
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 6: MANAGER_QUOTES is only used as fallback when managerVibe is undefined (line 371, 389). Since getTeamVibeKey always returns a valid key, VIBE_QUOTES always matches. MANAGER_QUOTES is dead code. Remove it.

suggestions.title: DEFAULT_ROSTER still in Office.jsx — should be in constants.js
suggestions.description: ROLE_EMOJIS and ROLE_COLORS were extracted to utils/constants.js but DEFAULT_ROSTER was not. Move it for consistency. Carried forward from #18.

suggestions.title: Header date doesn't use PST timezone
suggestions.description: App.jsx line ~597: new Date().toLocaleDateString('en-US', { weekday, month, day }) — uses viewer's local timezone. Near midnight, non-PST viewers see wrong date. The greeting was already fixed to use Intl with America/Los_Angeles — do the same for the date.

suggestions.title: WindowElement re-renders every 60s even when hour hasn't changed
suggestions.description: Office.jsx: setInterval fires every 60s calling setHour(getHour()). Since the hour changes once per hour, 59 of 60 calls are wasted renders. Minor but easy fix: compare before setting.

suggestions.title: No loading state for activity log
suggestions.description: Activity log shows 'No activity yet…' during initial fetch, which is misleading — it implies there IS no activity. Should show a skeleton or spinner during isLoading, then 'No activity yet…' only if the fetch succeeds with 0 entries.

suggestions.title: Activity filter buttons lack group label for screen readers
suggestions.description: The activity filter bar has role='group' and aria-label which is good, but individual filter buttons lack aria-pressed or aria-selected state indication. They use className 'active' but screen readers don't announce CSS classes. Add aria-pressed={activityFilter === key}.

suggestions.title: Ghost characters have no keyboard interaction feedback
suggestions.description: Ghost characters (roster members not currently working) have tabIndex={0} but onMouseEnter/onBlur only shows the OOO bubble for manager/working variants. Ghosts show a bubble but it's triggered via useState ghostBubble with a random timer — keyboard focus doesn't trigger it. Minor since ghosts are decorative.

suggestions.title: Worker card flip state persists when worker changes status
suggestions.description: WorkerCard uses local useState for isFlipped. If a worker goes from working to idle while the card is flipped, the back still shows. The card should auto-unflip when the worker's status changes.

suggestions.title: No way to see historical score trends
suggestions.description: Sofia's been reviewing for 19 cycles. It would be valuable to show a simple sparkline or score history on the dashboard. Not urgent, but a nice-to-have for tracking improvement over time.
